zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnès des Baux於大約1085年出生在法國普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o萊斯 包-德-普洛福斯時，她的父親Guillaume Hugues des Baux40歲，母親Vierne DE POSQUIERES26歲。她於大約1109年與Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet在法國奧其坦尼奧德納博訥結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1140年在法國奧其坦尼奧德納博訥享年56歲，埋葬在法國巴-諾門迪奧內沙吉艾福盧吉德-蒙特福特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnès des Baux于大约1085年出生在法国普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o萊斯 包-德-普洛福斯时，她的父亲Guillaume Hugues des Baux40岁，母亲Vierne DE POSQUIERES26岁。她于大约1109年与Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet在法国奧其坦尼奧德納博訥结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1140年在法国奧其坦尼奧德納博訥享年56岁，埋葬在法国巴-諾門迪奧內沙吉艾福盧吉德-蒙特福特。

nameMeanings.name: des
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (Alsace and Lorraine): from a shortened form of the German personal name   Debus  .

variants.name: Debus
meanings: German: from a personal name, a vernacular short form of Matthäus (see Matthew ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine); compare Debs .
French: habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone from any of various places called Bus (from a regional form of bois ‘wood’) in Eure, Pas-de-Calais, and Somme.

nameMeanings.name: Baux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Indian (mainly West Bengal) and Bangladeshi: from Bengali baul, Sanskrit vatula ‘madman’. Bearers of this name traditionally lived a lifestyle characterized by isolation from society, typically singing of social inequalities and injustice. They subsisted mainly on alms, considering themselves to be outside any organized religion, representing instead a polymorphic spiritual tradition.
German: relationship name from a term denoting a relative or close friend, from Middle High German buole, Middle Low German bole ‘relative, lover, close friend’.
German: possibly also from a rare variant of the personal name   Paul  .

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Agnès
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnès des Baux was born about 1085, in Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, her father, Guillaume Hugues des Baux, was 40 and her mother, Vierne DE POSQUIERES, was 26. She married Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet about 1109, in Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1140, in Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, France, at the age of 56, and was buried in Saint-Évroult-de-Montfort, Orne, Lower Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnès des Baux est née environ 1085, à Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, son père, Guillaume Hugues des Baux, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Vierne DE POSQUIERES, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet environ 1109, à Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1140, à Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, France, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint-Évroult-de-Montfort, Orne, Basse-Normandie, France.

Sie hat um 1109 in Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, Frankreich Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1140 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, Frankreich und wurde in Saint-Évroult-de-Montfort, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnès des Bauxが約1085年にフランスプローベンセ-アルペス-クoト ド'アズルボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンレス バウクス-デ-プローベンスで生まれた時，父Guillaume Hugues des Bauxは40歳，母Vierne DE POSQUIERESは26歳であった。約1109年にフランスオクシタニーアウドナルボンでRaymond III de Narbonne-Peletと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1140年にフランスオクシタニーアウドナルボンで56歳で死去し，フランスバセ-ノーマンディオルンサイント-evロウルト-デ-モントフォートにて埋葬された。

ko.Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône,: 프랑스 프로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 레스 바스-데-프로븐스
ko.lifeSummary: Agnès des Baux(이)가 대략 1085년 프랑스 프로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 레스 바스-데-프로븐스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume Hugues des Baux(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Vierne DE POSQUIERES(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1109년 프랑스 옥시탠이 오드 나르보니에서 Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1140년 프랑스 옥시탠이 오드 나르보니에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른 세인트-e브루트-데-몽포트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnès des Baux nasceu aproximadamente 1085, em Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, França, seu pai, Guillaume Hugues des Baux, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Vierne DE POSQUIERES, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet aproximadamente 1109, em Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1140, em Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, França, com 56 anos, e foi sepultada em Saint-Évroult-de-Montfort, Orne, Basse-Normandie, França.

ru.Les Baux-de-Provence, Bouches-du-Rhône,: лесь баукс-де-провенс, боучес-ду-рoн, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnès des Baux родилась около 1085 г. в местности лесь баукс-де-провенс, боучес-ду-рoн, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume Hugues des Baux было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Vierne DE POSQUIERES, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet около 1109 г. в местности нарбонн, ауд, окситани, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1140 г. в местности нарбонн, ауд, окситани, Франция в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности сеинт-eвроулт-де-монтфорт, орн, бассе-норманди, Франция.

Se casó con Raymond III de Narbonne-Pelet aproximadamente 1109, en Narbonne, Aude, Occitanie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija.
